commonIssues:
Applications are frequently rejected due to missing information (e.g., the mother's name of the deceased) or incorrect details.

How to fix:

  • Carefully review the GHMC Death Registration Form requirements before submitting — all mandatory fields including mother's name must be filled.
  • Obtain the deceased's hospital records, Aadhaar, voter ID to accurately fill in all family details.
  • Apply via TS MeeSeva Portal or visit your nearest MeeSeva Centre.
  • Helpline: 1800-599-4788 (Telangana e-Services)

A recurring issue is that the digital key required by officials (AMOH) to approve certificates either expires or is not renewed, causing a halt in certificate generation.

How to fix:

  • Confirm with your local GHMC or Municipal office whether the AMOH digital key has expired.
  • If expired, request the office to escalate to the GHMC Commissioner's office or the Director of Municipal Administration for immediate key renewal.
  • Raise a formal complaint via Telangana Grievance Portal citing the specific office and the digital key issue.
  • Helpline: 1800-599-4788

Applicants report technical glitches on the MeeSeva site and issues with digitization of records, preventing new applications from being registered properly.

How to fix:

  • Use the TS MeeSeva Portal during off-peak hours with Chrome browser.
  • Visit a different MeeSeva centre in a nearby area if your local centre has persistent issues.
  • For digitization issues (old records not found), request the MeeSeva operator to contact the backend CDMA office for record verification.
  • Escalate to: 1800-599-4788 (Telangana e-Governance)

If a death is not registered within 21 days, the process becomes complicated. After one year, approval from the Revenue Divisional Officer (RDO) is required.

How to fix:

  • Within 21 days: Register at GHMC or local ULB office — free of charge.
  • 21 days to 1 year: Submit a Notarised Affidavit with a late fee at the local municipal office.
  • After 1 year: Apply to the Revenue Divisional Officer (RDO) with supporting documents and a Notarised Affidavit. After RDO approval, register at the ULB.
  • Apply at: TS MeeSeva Portal.

Applications often pile up at local GHMC offices (e.g., Musheerabad circle), causing long wait times. Even after GHMC staff verification, final certificate issuance is often delayed.

How to fix:

  • Track your application at TS MeeSeva Portal using your acknowledgement number.
  • If delayed beyond 15 working days after field verification, escalate to the Zonal Commissioner of GHMC in writing.
  • Raise a grievance at Telangana Grievance Portal.
  • Contact GHMC helpline: 040-2131-1111 for Hyderabad cases.
